Maddeyle çevreyle uyum teminatı, teminat mektubu verilmiş olan ruhsatlarda maddenin yürürlük tarihinden itibaren herhangi bir bildirim yapılmaksızın bir yıl içerisinde teminat mektuplarındaki kayıtlı tutar kadar bedelin MAPEG hesabına yatırılma zorunluluğu getirilmiştir. Maddeyle rehabilitasyon bedelinin işletme ruhsat sahibinden işletme ruhsat bedeli kadar her yıl alınacağı şeklinde yeni bir düzenleme dikkate alınarak işletme ruhsat bedelinin yüzde 30'u oranında bir indirim amaçlanmaktadır.
Kanunun
14'üncü maddesi önemli maddelerimizden bir tanesi. Bu maddeyle de, 14'üncü maddesiyle de yenilenebilir enerji projelerinde mera alanlarının tahsis süreçleri düzenlenmektedir. Bakanlıkça Yenilenebilir Enerji Kaynak Alanı (YEKA) olarak ilan edilen yerlerde yapılacak YEKA Projeleri için gerekli olan mera alanlarının tahsis amacı değişikliğine ilişkin Bakanlıkça ilgili idare nezdinde işlem yürütülebilmesi ve hem bu projeler için hem diğer enerji projeleri için mera tahsis amacı değişikliği işlemi ile çevresel etki değerlendirmesi sürecinin eş zamanlı olarak sürdürülebilmesi maksadıyla mera tahsis amacı değişikliği başvurularında ÇED raporunun ön şart olarak istenmemesi sağlanmaktadır.
BAŞKAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Oğuzhan Bey...
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Düzenleme kapsamında yapılması öngörülen yeni düzenlemeyle bu süreçte önemli bir dönüşüm hedeflenmektedir. YEKA'da belirlenen alanlarda mera tahsis amacı değişikliğine yönelik işlemlerin, yarışma yapılmadan önce, Enerji ve Tabii Kaynaklar Bakanlığı koordinasyonunda ilgili idareler nezdinde yürütülmesine imkân tanınmaktadır. Bu sayede, yatırımcılara önceden vasfı değiştirilmiş, kullanıma hazır alanların sunulması mümkün hâle getirilecek ve bu projelerin uygulanabilirliği önemli ölçüde artırılacaktır.
Sonuç olarak, yapılacak bu değişiklikle yenilenebilir enerji yatırımlarının hızlandırılması hedeflenmekte, Türkiye’nin enerji dönüşüm hedeflerine ulaşmasında stratejik bir katkı sağlayacaktır. Enerji arz güvenliği, çevresel sürdürülebilirlik ve ekonomik kalkınma açısından büyük önem taşıyan bir durumdur.
Yine, kanunun 15'inci maddesiyle izin başvurularının azami altmış gün içinde sonuçlandırılması, ön lisans döneminde alınan iznin lisans süresi sonuna kadar geçerli olması, etüt aşamasındaki işler için ayrıca izin aranmaması hedeflenmektedir.

Rüzgâr ve güneş enerjisine dayalı yenilenebilir enerji tesislerinin imar ve yapı ruhsatı işlemlerinin Enerji ve Tabii Kaynaklar Bakanlığı tarafından da yapılabilmesi için madde düzenlenmiştir. Mevzuata göre enerji üretim tesislerinin imar planları; Çevre, Şehircilik ve İklim Değişikliği Bakanlığı, il özel idareleri ve ilgili yerel yönetimler tarafından onaylanmakta, yapı ruhsatı ve yapı kullanma izin belgeleri ise esasen yerel yönetimlerce düzenlenmektedir. Ancak uygulamada, bu süreçler kurumlar arası koordinasyon eksiklikleri, teknik kapasite farklılıkları ve uygulama birliği sağlanamaması gibi nedenlerle ciddi gecikmelere yol açmaktadır. Söz konusu onay ve ruhsatların edinilmesi zaman zaman bir yılı aşan sürelerde gerçekleşmektedir. Yatırımcı açısından bu durum, süreçlerin öngörülemez hâle gelmesine ve yatırım takvimlerinin aksamasına neden olmaktadır. Bu durumu kısaltmak için bu düzenlemeyi yapıyoruz. Düzenleme kapsamında, belediyelerin ya da özel idarelerin yetkileri alınmıyor, alternatif olarak Bakanlığa da bu yetki verilmiş oluyor.
17'nci maddeyle de Kanuna geçici 4'üncü maddesine yeni fıkra ekleyerek burada süre değişikliği yapıyoruz. “31/12/2020” ifadesini “31/12/2030” şeklinde ve “on yıl” ibaresini “on beş yıl” şeklinde değiştiriyoruz. Kanunun yayımı tarihinden itibaren 31/12/2030 tarihine kadar işletmeye girecek olan ve Bakanlık tarafından düzenlenen maden işletme ruhsatı ve izni kapsamında 3213 sayılı Kanun'un 2'nci maddesinin IV. Grup (b) bendinde yer alan madenlerin girdi olarak kullanıldığı elektrik üretim tesislerinden, ulaşım yollarından ve lisanslarında belirtilen sisteme bağlantı noktasına kadarki TEİAŞ ve dağıtım şirketlerine devredilecek olanlar da dâhil enerji nakil hatlarından, lisans tarihinden itibaren on beş yıl boyunca izin, kira, irtifak hakkı ve kullanma izni bedellerine yüzde 85 indirim uygulaması şeklinde bu süreyi uzatmış oluyoruz.
BAŞKAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Sayın Vekilim, ilk imza sahibisiniz diye çok müsamaha gösterdik ama toparlayalım.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Başkanım, son...
Burada da yine aynı şekilde burada da beş yıl uzatma getiriyoruz. Burada da yenilenebilir enerji için uzatma getiriyoruz aynı maddeyi...
Düzenleme gerekliliği yapılması, bunda da düzenleme yapıyoruz.
Başkanım, sabırla dinledikleri için herkese teşekkür ediyorum.
BAŞKAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Sayın Vekilim, ben de diğer milletvekillerimize sabırla dinledikleri için teşekkür ediyorum.
Tabii, artık yeni dönemde ilk imza sahibi önemli olduğu için size biraz pozitif ayrımcılık yapmış olduk. Çok teşekkür ediyorum. Kanun çalışmalarında da diğer ekiplerle nasıl çalıştığınızı biliyoruz.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Teşekkür ediyoruz Başkanım.
records.englishTranslation:
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Dear President, esteemed members of parliament, our Deputy Minister, our bureaucrats, representatives of very valuable non-governmental organizations, very valuable members of the press and all present; We have gathered to discuss our Bill No. 95 dated 13/6/2005 on Amendments to Certain Laws. I greet you all with respect, love and respect.
I hope that peace will be established in the region and the oppression will end as soon as possible, and that Israel will end all its actions that threaten regional peace, especially its genocide against the people of Gaza, and I condemn its latest attacks against Iran.
The Turkish Century vision is not just a development program, it is the institutional, economic and strategic basis of the ideal of a great and powerful Türkiye. One of the most important pillars of this vision is undoubtedly complete independence in energy. Energy security is no longer just an economic issue; It is the basis of national sovereignty, development and international competitiveness. Energy and natural resources are not only economic, but also the fundamental basis of our strategic independence, production power and international competitive capacity. This proposal is not just a legal regulation, it is a transition to a new phase in Türkiye's energy and mining strategy.
We have spent 1 trillion dollars on energy imports since 2022. While our energy and mining imports were 96.5 billion dollars in 2022, this figure decreased to 65.6 billion dollars in 2024. Our installed power, which was 31,846 megawatts in 2002, has reached 118,668 megawatts as of April 2024. The targeted installed capacity for 2035 is 227,200 megawatts.
In the Türkiye National Energy Plan announced in 2022, a target was set to increase the rate of electricity production from renewable energy sources, which is currently 45 percent, to 65 percent by 2035. Achieving this target depends especially on the increase in wind and solar investments, and renewable energy projects must be implemented quickly to achieve the target. With the regulations made in the proposal, it is envisaged that the permit processes for these projects will be made simpler, simpler and faster in order to reach the target within a reasonable time.
Although there has been a significant increase in investments in renewable energy resources, these investments need to be increased and continued within the scope of the Green Transformation process. By the end of 2024, the total installed power in Türkiye has reached 115.9 gigawatts, of which 69 gigawatts, or 60 percent, are renewable energy sources. 19.8 gigawatts of this is solar, and 12.8 gigawatts is wind-based power plants.
In 2024, 159.4 terawatt hours, or 45 percent, of the 349 terawatt hours of electricity production was produced from renewable resources, thus preventing the import of approximately 32 billion cubic meters of natural gas and preventing a foreign trade deficit of approximately 12 billion US dollars. Similarly, if the same production was met by imported coal,
At average prices in 2004, approximately 68 million tons of coal worth 7.7 billion US dollars would have been imported.

As can be seen, the use of renewable resources is not only a need for green transformation, but also has a strategic importance in reducing foreign dependency on energy and reducing the energy-related current account deficit. With the proposal, it is aimed to simplify the permit and approval processes, which currently take forty-eight months, to less than twenty-four months, in order to achieve the renewable energy targets determined in the Turkish National Energy Plan without delay, and to accelerate the green transformation in energy in our country and to ensure sustainable development by reducing emissions.
Our country's energy demand is increasing every year due to economic growth, industrialization and population growth. Electricity demand in 2024 was realized at 349 terawatt hours, and the fact that electricity consumption, which was approximately 211 terawatt hours in 2010, has increased by 65 percent in the last fourteen years, reveals that the increase in our country's energy needs will continue. In addition, with the projections made, it is predicted that the need for electrical energy will increase by 4 percent annually and will reach 510 terawatt hours by 2035. In order to meet this increase and ensure energy supply security, a production portfolio based on domestic resources, sustainable and consisting of diverse resources is vital. In this context, electricity production based on domestic and renewable resources comes to the fore as a strategic element of Türkiye's energy supply security policy.
Approximately 47.3 terawatt hours, or 14 percent, of electricity production in 2024 was obtained from domestic coal power plants. In this way, the import of approximately 9 billion cubic meters of natural gas was prevented and a foreign trade deficit of approximately 3.5 billion US dollars was prevented. Therefore, electricity generation based on domestic coal is not only a technical production choice but also an indispensable element in terms of economic stability, national interest and energy independence.
On the other hand, renewable energy sources have an intermittent production character by nature. Electricity is produced from these sources in sunny and non-windy periods, and from hydraulic resources in dry periods. This situation makes frequency and voltage stabilization difficult in the communication system and creates a need for a flexible and powerful base-load source to meet sudden production drops. At this point, domestic coal power plants ensure that the system balance is maintained thanks to their ability to produce continuously. These power plants undertake a critical task with their flexible operational structures to balance variable production from renewable resources. Especially in winter months, when renewable production is low, these power plants meet the main load of the grid. A striking example of this situation was seen with the widespread power outages in Spain and Portugal on April 28, 2025. At a time when production from renewable resources was intense, production facilities were disabled, and this incident once again showed the importance of energy resources that can produce continuous and reliable production in the system.

Thanks to their flexible structure and stable operation, base load power plants play an important role in preventing such imbalances. In order to prevent a similar incident from occurring in Türkiye and to ensure that the transmission system continues to operate safely, it is of great importance that resources capable of reliable and continuous production continue to exist in the system. Base-load power plants provide balance to the system with their continuously operating structures and ensure the continuity of electricity supply against sudden production drops. In a structure where renewable production is increasingly increasing, continuing to operate these power plants in an efficient and controlled manner has become a strategic necessity for both the operational security of the system and the security of energy supply.
Based on the above-mentioned issues, it is aimed to make a temporary regulation to eliminate the danger of closure of existing domestic coal-fired thermal power plants, which are of great importance for electricity supply security and price stability, due to raw material supply problems.
It is known that approximately 90 types of mines are produced and operated economically in the world today.
Our country, which can produce approximately 60 types of minerals, ranks at the top among the world countries in this regard with its mineral diversity. Our country's mineral reserve potential is quite high, but despite this mineral diversity and reserve potential we have, the share of the mining sector in the gross domestic product is around 1.2 percent. In the Twelfth Development Plan, it is aimed to increase this rate to 2 percent by 2028. It is known that this rate is between 5 and 8 percent in developed countries.
With the proposal, it is aimed to carry out mining activities more effectively and efficiently by increasing environmental and human sensitivity by regulating the provisions regarding the rehabilitation works to be carried out in the areas where mining activities are carried out, to increase the predictability in the permit processes for mineral exploration and operation activities and to make the permit periods more reasonable, to protect the vested rights of investors, to carry out and encourage exploration activities with more qualified methods, and to take additional measures to fulfill the commitments made by the investor during the operation phase.
Considering the economic magnitude of the investments required by mining activities, legal predictability is as essential as market predictability in order to realize these investments. Permit processes in mining constitute one of the most critical elements of this predictability. In practice, it is seen that the permit processes cannot be completed in a reasonable time, and considering the operating license periods, a large part of these periods are spent in the permit stages and production can be started after many years. For these reasons, planning mining investments becomes difficult and mining ceases to be an attractive investment area. The biggest indicators of this situation are the decrease in the number of established investors in our country, unlike developed countries, the low number of investors and the decrease in the number of license requests.

In the proposal prepared by taking all these issues into consideration, the permit processes in mining have been rearranged. Accordingly, the permit processes for the proposals will be completed before the license is issued by the MAPEG General Directorate. It is envisaged to accelerate investments by increasing coordination between environmental impact assessment processes and permit processes. On the other hand, in case of conflict between institutions, the 4th group aims to grant permits for strategic or critical mines by the board formed under the chairmanship of the Vice President and with the participation of relevant ministers, considering the superior public interest.
On the other hand, not making regulations specific to strategic and critical mines, which can cause serious economic problems and security vulnerabilities in case of supply interruption and high price increases, whose importance increases with technological developments in terms of their field of use, which meets the raw material needs of the industry, which is used for military purposes, which plays a critical role in national security and economic prosperity, and which causes a large current account deficit, is not in line with national interests and public interest. The proposal aims to determine the legal framework of strategic and critical minerals and to introduce the possibility of urgent expropriation and stockpiling obligation for these mines.
As it is known, as a result of mineral exploration activities, underground mineral reserves can be determined with their grade, density and three dimensions, thus the operating activity process can be designed and the economic results of mining investments can be predicted. In this respect, exploration activities constitute the first and important link of mining processes. In the proposal, an investment guarantee requirement is introduced for the mineral exploration license regulation, and mineral exploration activities, which by their nature require significant labor, capital, technical knowledge and equipment, are carried out by investors who have economic power and technically sufficient criteria, thus increasing the quality of mineral exploration activities in our country, recording the guarantee as income in case the investments committed during the exploration license period are not realized, sanctioning license cancellation in case the minimum exploration activities are not carried out and preventing exploration license areas from being left idle, thanks to these regulations, exploration activities are made more efficient. It is aimed to be done effectively and more efficiently.
Lack of effective operating activities in operating license areas in the current situation
It is seen that the mines can be left idle as a result of not complying with the annual production commitments declared in the project. However, it is evaluated that the minimum production rates in the twelfth paragraph of the 24th article of the Mining Law No. 3213 dated 4/6/1985, which stipulates that administrative sanctions will be imposed in case the production amounts declared in the operation project are not fulfilled to a certain extent, are also low to meet the targets of our country.
The proposal introduces a requirement for production activity at minimum standards in order for requests for extension of the license period to be deemed appropriate.

In this context, bringing minerals into our country's economy; It is aimed to ensure supply security and reduce foreign dependency in terms of industry, raw materials and mines that meet the country's electricity needs.
Studies regarding environmental compatibility in areas where mining activities are carried out are generally carried out after the license law is finalized in line with the relevant provisions of Law No. 3213. Mining activities based on a license in line with the relevant provisions of the law continue for more than thirty years. In the aforementioned law, it is stated that 30 percent of the operating license fees collected for these studies can be used, and there is no provision stating that environmental adaptation studies can be carried out while mining activities continue in the field and these fees can be accrued in a separate account. In the current situation, the environmental compliance fees collected from license holders cannot even cover the project and cost of environmental adaptation studies that need to be carried out at the end of mining activities and disrupt environmental harmonization efforts.
The proposal aims to make the areas involved in mining activities effective, efficient and environmentally compatible as quickly as possible by increasing the rehabilitation fee and determining new obligations to ensure compliance with the rehabilitation obligation.
I would like to briefly talk about the regulations and articles we made within the scope of the proposal.
In our article 1, the prerequisite for applying to other permit and approval processes before an environmental impact assessment decision is taken is eliminated. In addition, the decision "Environmental Impact Assessment is Not Required" is abolished.
The current problem here is that one of the main problems encountered in energy investments is that the "EIA is not Required" decision given by the governorships is perceived by the public as not being carried out by the relevant stakeholders. This situation creates the impression among both the public, non-governmental organizations and judicial authorities that environmental risks are ignored, and makes it difficult to properly understand technical processes. In the planned situation after the regulation is made, the statement "Environmental Impact Assessment is Not Required" is removed and a single concept and document structure is adopted for all projects subject to the environmental impact assessment process. Thus, it will be clearly emphasized that no matter what result is reached, each project goes through an environmental review process and the necessary commitments and measures are determined at the end of this period. With this change, the environmental impact assessment process will be made more transparent and understandable; It will be more clearly demonstrated that the decisions taken are based on technical foundations and environmental sensitivity is taken into account. In addition, with the regulation, it will be possible to carry out other permit mechanisms related to the environmental assessment process simultaneously, thus providing significant time and savings in investor processes.

The new approach offers a decision structure that clearly states that environmental impacts are not ignored, but are evaluated in scientific and administrative processes. In this way, environmental protection principles will be strengthened, delays in investments will be prevented and will contribute to the economic development process.
With the regulation to be made; The quality of environmental assessments is increased, processes are simplified, and we contribute to accurately informing the public. Prepared with the understanding that environmental sensitivity is not a factor that hinders the pace of investment, but a tool that strengthens the process when managed correctly, this amendment serves the goals of both protecting the environment and accelerating investments at the same time.
"If the EIA process is negative, wouldn't canceling the other permit given as a result of the negative EIA process cause investors to lose time and resources?" A question may come to our mind: If a negative decision is made at the end of the EIA process, other applications for permits related to the project will also result negatively. Therefore, processes carried out simultaneously are subject to the results of the EIA. The aim is to save time and speed up the process for projects that are expected to yield positive results. If negative results are obtained, investors will act accordingly. Investors will act knowing the possible risks and consequences from the beginning of the processes, and this will allow investors to perform more effective risk management. In addition, investors who are aware of this risk will develop their projects more carefully and meticulously.
We introduce some definitions regarding rehabilitation with the 2nd article of the Law and the 3rd article of the Mining Law. We introduce definitions such as license fee, board, rehabilitation; also the education office. Since the article proposes detailed regulations regarding the rehabilitation of mining areas, we have added the necessary definitions of board, rehabilitation, rehabilitation fee, account and collection office accordingly.
Currently, in accordance with Law No. 3213, 30 percent of license fees are allocated to environmental compliance activities. This fee is insufficient for environmental compliance activities, and the provisions regarding environmental compliance in Law No. 3213 do not meet the needs of the day. In the Law No. 3213, it is regulated that receivables such as state right license fee and expenses that are not paid within the due date will be followed and collected by the collection office in accordance with the Law No. 6183 on the Collection Procedure of Public Receivables, but this process is not carried out since the relevant collection office is not defined.
Licensing of areas where it is not clear whether a permit will be obtained in accordance with the provisions of Article 7, which regulates the permits required for mining activities; It poses a risk for investors, strengthens the orientation of local and foreign investors to the mining sector, and causes tender prices to remain low.
Whether or not the sites are abandoned in an environmentally friendly manner negatively affects the perspective on the mining industry. This perspective subsequently prevents the development of mining activities.

Considering the mineral diversity and reserves in our country, the share of the mining sector in the gross domestic product is low compared to developed countries. In the 4th group of mines, in order to ensure that disputes regarding the permits required for strategic and critical mines can be resolved by common will without resorting to judicial bodies, differences of interpretation regarding the implementation of the laws will be set aside and resolved quickly and inexpensively within the framework of the principle of public interest in accordance with the provisions of the Constitution. In this way, a board institution that will ensure predictability for investors is introduced by regulation.
Rehabilitation activities are carried out as works within the scope of making the topography damaged due to mining activities and changing areas safe, arranging, ensuring sensitivity, correcting, laying topsoil, planting seeds, planting saplings, creating recreation areas in places with suitable land structure, planting, afforestation, chemical and physical improvement. With the new rehabilitation provisions, the license holder will be charged a rehabilitation fee as well as the operating fee.
With the proposal, the part of the license fee transferred to the budgets of the administrations - 70 percent - is kept at the same rate. The environmental compatibility fee, which constitutes 30 percent of the license fee, is regulated as a new payment item in the 6th article of the framework, thus the environmental compatibility fee is increased from 30 percent to 70 percent under the name of "rehabilitation".
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan, are there more?
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Yes, President, a lot.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - In fact, we can speed up a little.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, let me tell you all, then there will be no questions left in your mind.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Well, let's see.
Here you go, continue, Mr. Oğuzhan.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Let's give an example: Currently, when we receive 100 liras, 30 percent of it goes to the rehabilitation fee and 30 percent goes to MAPEG. With the change, we add a rehabilitation fee equal to the license fee, and therefore 30 units are taken from the license fee. We make the regulation as a separate item, not within the license fee. We also regulate that the amounts received for rehabilitation will be kept in a special account opened with public banks to be used for rehabilitation expenses on behalf of the relevant administration.
Pursuant to the law, for the receivables to be pursued and collected in accordance with Law No. 6183, the Investment Monitoring Coordination Directorate and the relevant collection unit in the special provincial administration are authorized for the receivables to be pursued and collected in accordance with the Law No.
In addition, a new permit system is envisaged with the proposed amendment to Article 7, which regulates the permits required for mining activities. Accordingly, in cases where group 4 and strategic or critical mines are not permitted by the relevant institution, the final decision on the permit will be made by the board.

With the proposed definition, the basic principles regarding the formation of the board and the decision-making procedure are determined and it is stipulated that the working procedures and principles will be regulated by the Presidential regulation.
With the 3rd article of our proposal, we are rearranging the permit processes in mining activities by amending the 7th article of the Mining Law. The aim of the proposal is to notify the General Directorate of the area coordinates for which permission is required in line with other legal provisions in order to carry out mining activities more efficiently, to rearrange the mining permit processes, to first obtain permission from the relevant institutions for the project to be prepared for the area subject to the license, and to continue the permission in that area in the following processes.
Why did we feel such a need? After the license is granted, the permits that must be obtained for mining activities in accordance with Article 7 of the Mining Law No. 3213 lead to bureaucratic processes that exceed reasonable periods in practice, and this causes a significant portion of the operating license periods to be passed without production. Lack of predictability in permit processes causes serious economic investments to go unrewarded, and the principle of legal security and acquired rights are damaged. Due to bureaucratic blockage and unpredictability in permit processes, mining investments cannot be planned properly and the sector loses its attractiveness. Different approaches of different ministries and institutions in the permit processes lead to incompatibility between administrations, and in the current system, decisions cannot be made taking into account the public interest. With the regulation we have made, the permit processes have been reorganized in a way that is predictable and respects the principle of legal security. The current bureaucratic functioning alienates investors from the sector and causes short-term, inefficient investments; These have been edited.
The obligation to notify the relevant institutions about the areas subject to permit before issuing a license has been expanded and coordination between administrations has been ensured. A new permit system was established, permit processes were carried out by MAPEG, and the rule that if these permits were not granted within four months, they were deemed to have been granted, thus accelerating the permit processes. In addition, the principle that the permission given is considered valid as long as the license law continues. The principle of granting a free permit to MAPEG for twenty-four months before the license in state forests, operating the free permit given, receiving fees and commitments in case it is transferred to real or legal persons or made to operate, and the first permits given are considered valid as long as the license law continues. Permission processes have been accelerated by introducing the rule that if these permits are not granted within four months, they will be deemed to have been granted. The permitted area will be under the control of MAPEG as long as the license law continues. Continuing mining activities in areas that later became subject to permission, preserving the license as an acquired right, but identifying cultural assets in the field and mining
This article provides for the cancellation of the license by paying compensation if a favorable opinion is not given for its activities.

During the EIA process, it has been regulated that the institutions that initially gave permission cannot give a negative opinion later, and it is also envisaged to accelerate the permit processes by introducing legal predictability and the rule that if the administrations that have not previously received permission in this process do not give their opinions within four months, they will be deemed to have been given permission. It is envisaged that if permission is not given by the institution regarding the 4th group mines and strategic and critical mines, the final decision will be made by the board chaired by the Vice President.
The general purpose of the article is to increase mining investments and investment security, and to simplify and accelerate permit processes.
A question like this may come to mind: Are there any examples in other laws where permission is deemed to have been granted if the administration does not grant permission in due time? There is such a regulation in Article 93 of the Banking Law No. 5411. Again, there are regulations regarding this in other laws.
Does MAPEG have the capacity to fulfill the duties assigned to it by this law? Considering the possibility of the proposal becoming law.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Is there such a law? No one answers, and by not answering they are freed from responsibility.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Dear Deputy, when you have the right to speak, you speak and say these things.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - You cannot interfere with us.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - I'm making a presentation here, why can't I get involved?
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - You cannot interfere with when I will answer.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - You cannot break my harmony either.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Friends, please.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - You only tell, you cannot interfere.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Why can't I get involved? Am I a maraba here? I am a member of parliament, why can't I get involved?
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - You cannot decide when I will speak.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - You can talk whenever you want, you cannot interfere with me.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Friends, please.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Yes.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - You can't.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Yes.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - You can't.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Friends, let's not argue with each other.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - The President interferes, you cannot interfere; You just tell.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - You cannot intervene.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Please, Mr. Deputy.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - The President can interfere, you cannot interfere.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - I can interfere, why shouldn't I?
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Please, let's not interfere with the orator.
Mr. Oğuzhan, you continue.
Here you go.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - No institution will respond, even by not responding.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Friends.
AHMET TUNCAY ÖZKAN (İzmir) - As the proposer, what do you say to the MP?
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - It gives adjustments.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - I say you can't break my harmony, what else should I say? If we are the bidder, we are also members of parliament here.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Friends.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - You came here to explain your topic, not to make adjustments.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan, continue.
Friends, please be respectful to the orator and follow the etiquette of discussion.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Mr. President, first of all the orator will be respectful.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - But you are intervening, Mr. Deputy.
AHMET TUNCAY ÖZKAN (İzmir) - Will do.

MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Why will he do it? Is there such a rule? Then everyone should intervene with everyone else. So is there such a method?
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Aren't we going to express our opinion?
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - Tell me when I give you my word. I will give you my word, Mr. Deputy, then you can tell me.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - OK, thank you.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - Here you go, Mr. Oğuzhan.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Similar regulations are also included in other legislation. Issues such as Article 30 of the Zoning Law No. 3194, Article 7 of the Law No. 4706 on the Evaluation of Treasury-Owned Immovable Properties and Amendments to the Value Added Tax Law, and building occupancy permits are also related to this.
With the 4th article of the law, the repealed article 8 of the Mining Law is re-established and regulations regarding strategic and critical mines are made. The article envisages that some minerals will be designated as strategic or critical minerals by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources, taking into account the national and international supply and demand situation, usage areas, sector needs, import and export levels, and taking the opinions of relevant ministry institutions and organizations. With the regulation, the permit processes for strategic and critical mines are subject to the permit processes in Article 7, and in case the permit is not granted, it is aimed to be submitted to the board for permit by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources.
Although approximately 90 types of mines are operated in the world, not all of these mines have the same importance and priority. In the Mining Law No. 3213, there are general regulations regarding the group and type of minerals in terms of exploration and operation activities.
and, with the exception of a few exceptional regulations, different provisions based on group, type and mine are envisaged for the execution of these activities. Mining groups that do not have the same importance are subject to the same legal regulation; It may cause a national security weakness, failure to implement the right policies in terms of current account deficit and economic growth, and serious risks in terms of industrial production.
In parallel with the developments in the world, the geopolitical location of our country, technological developments, especially in the defense industry, and similar reasons, it is gaining importance to bring strategic and critical minerals into the economy. Precautions must be taken to ensure the continuity of supply of these minerals, to take precautions against supply interruptions, to create and implement economic policies, to quickly identify and extract the mineral needs of the industry, to bring them into our economy and to stock them.
Mines that are among the inputs of industrial production that may cause serious economic problems and security vulnerabilities in case of supply interruption and high price increases and that have a high supply risk are defined as "critical mines", while mines that are of high importance for national security and economic prosperity and whose supply can be restricted by internal and external forces are defined as "strategic mines".

It is envisaged that strategic and critical minerals will be determined by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources in line with the opinions of the Ministry of National Defense, the Ministry of Industry and Technology, the Ministry of Commerce and relevant public institutions and organizations.
The possibility of urgent expropriation is introduced in expropriation transactions regarding strategic and critical mines. The President is also given the authority to determine the mineral stock by taking into account the needs of the country in possible risky situations. Thus, thanks to the proposed regulation, special regulations regarding our minerals of special importance in terms of public interest, especially rare earth elements, are brought into a legal process. There are similar examples of this in the world. Countries such as the EU, USA, Japan and Australia, which determine their own strategic and critical raw materials, generally act according to various strategic goals such as national security, economic growth, technological development and sustainable development. For example, defense industries, production of electronic devices and energy-related raw materials are among the priorities of the USA. As a foreign-dependent country, Japan attaches great importance to the security of supply of strategic and critical raw materials, and Canada implements detailed incentive programs in this context.
With the 5th article of the law, it is envisaged that the income share of the operating license fee included in the 13th article of the Mining Law will be increased from 50 percent to 70 percent. Currently, 30 percent of the license fees are allocated to environmental compliance studies, 20 percent to the General Directorate budget, and 50 percent to the general budget. With the proposal, a 30 percent discount is made on license fees and it is envisaged that a rehabilitation fee will be collected in addition to the license fee. With the proposal, since it is envisaged to collect a rehabilitation fee equal to the operating license fee in future articles, the share to be transferred to the general budget is determined as 70 percent of the operating license fee, thus preventing a decrease in treasury revenues.
In addition, the article regulates issues such as collecting the rehabilitation fee from the owner of the raw material production permit, apart from public institutions and organizations, accruing interest from the rehabilitation fees, and administrative sanctions in case the provisions regarding the payment of the fees are not fulfilled.
Finally, public institutions and organizations are primarily and essentially obliged to undertake rehabilitation for which raw material production permits are granted, but if desired, the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry provides the opportunity to carry out the rehabilitation obligations on behalf of these institutions, provided that the necessary expenses are covered.
According to the law, 30 percent of the operating license fees are taken as collateral for environmental compliance studies. License holders can get back the environmental compliance fee they pay every year, provided that they make the site environmentally compatible. Due to the single treasury account application, it is not possible to accrue environmental compliance fees, to monitor the interest, and to submit the interest to the administrations that will carry out environmental adaptation studies before the works are carried out.

For this reason, the environmental compliance fees currently collected from license holders cannot be used in environmental compliance studies that need to be carried out after mining activities, they cannot cover the costs of these studies and environmental compliance studies are disrupted. Failure to abandon the sites in an environmentally friendly manner negatively affects the perspective on the mining industry, and this perspective continues
It prevents the development of mining activities. Considering the mineral diversity and reserves in our country, efforts are being made to increase the level of the mining sector within the gross national product.
The regulation in question was needed in order to carry out more effective work in rehabilitation works, thus strengthening the implementation of the principles of life and property safety and nature protection, increasing public support against mining, and as a result, mining activities can be carried out more effectively and in accordance with the principle of sustainable mining.
With the change in the calculation of the operating license fee, it has been regulated that the rehabilitation fee will be collected as much as the operating license fee. Parallelism with the provisions regarding the license fee has been achieved in the rehabilitation fee, maturity, payment time, collection procedure and sanction of non-payment. It has been ensured that rehabilitation fees can be kept in a special account, and it has been regulated that the rehabilitation fee cannot be transferred, seized or assigned by showing that it is a guarantee.
In order to prevent the loss of value of environmental and adaptation fees over the years, it has been regulated that the Ministry will charge interest in a way determined by the regulation, the rehabilitation fees will be returned to the license holder if the rehabilitation is carried out, the General Directorate may stop the mining production activities if the rehabilitation activity is not carried out properly, and those who own our raw material production, which is not under the responsibility of public institutions and organizations, have rehabilitation obligations.
Although it is regulated that raw material production permit holders belonging to public institutions and organizations have responsibilities regarding rehabilitation, it is also regulated that the rehabilitation will be carried out by the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry by transferring the resources they have allocated for rehabilitation to the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry. In addition, it has been stipulated that public institutions and organizations that violate their rehabilitation obligations will be given one month, and if the deficiency is not corrected, the activity will be stopped. It has also been regulated that the duties and powers of the General Directorate of Mining and Petroleum Affairs regarding rehabilitation will be used by the Investment Monitoring Coordination Directorate and special provincial administrations for the 1st group (a) subparagraph mines.
With the 6th article of the Law, the phrase "chrome" in subparagraph (e) of the fourth paragraph of the 14th article of the Mining Law is removed from the text of the paragraph, and it is regulated that a state right of 50 percent more than the license fee is obtained in the fields for operating permits. It is aimed to prevent the loss of state rights. Namely, the state right rate for gold is currently 2 in the table number 3 annexed to the Mining Law No. 3213.

For 101 ounces and above, it is 15 percent, and since the ounce price reached 2,910 dollars on average as of February 2005, the state share corresponding to this price is applied as 15 percent. The persistence of global economic uncertainties and the continuation of expansionary monetary policies by central banks reveal that gold prices will vary. For this reason, the state right has been increased for prices of 2,101 dollars per ounce and above. In addition, since there were expectations within the same framework for the other metallic minerals in Table 3, updates were needed for the other minerals in this table, and in this context, it was aimed to prevent loss of state rights with the change made in the additional table 3. On the other hand, although the chrome mine is included in table 3 annexed to the Mining Law No. 3213, it is not traded in the London Metal Exchange, so it is aimed to remove it from this table and make it subject to general provisions and to prevent loss of state rights. According to the current law, state rights are collected from mines every year at least equal to the operating license fee. According to the new model envisaged for the rehabilitation of mining areas, the license fee is reduced by 30 percent and the rehabilitation fee is eliminated. However, since the state right is received at least as much as the license fee, it is aimed that the reduction of the license fee will not cause a decrease in the state right income. In accordance with the law, state rights at least equal to the license fee are collected every year from state mining licenses with operating permits, and state rights and environmental compliance fees are collected for raw material production permit areas that are not under the responsibility of public institutions and organizations. With the new regulations, the calculation of the operating license fee has been changed.
10th of the law
Chromium was extracted from the substance, we talked about this.
With the 7th article of our bill, except for the 2nd group subparagraph (b) and 4th group mining licenses in the 16th article of the Mining Law, licenses can be licensed by tender in cases deemed necessary in line with field location, reserve information and similar issues, and the issues of obtaining investment guarantees in mineral exploration projects are regulated.
The article aims to ensure that real investors engage in qualified exploration activities by imposing the investment guarantee requirement during the mineral exploration period. In practice, it is seen that real or legal persons who can meet the minimum requirements for an exploration license but do not plan to engage in mineral exploration activities or are not financially and technically sufficient to do so can obtain an exploration license for different purposes. This causes the fields to remain idle in the hands of people who do not carry out qualified exploration activities.
Different mines in the same group cannot be licensed separately, even if they are located together in the same area. Especially since the extraction and operation of group 4 minerals require different technologies, investment costs and planning processes, carrying out different mining activities under a single license is not economically, environmentally and technically sustainable.

With the amendment in the seventh paragraph of Article 16, it is obligatory to pay an investment guarantee of 5 percent of the cost of the project submitted by the applicant in the work program in order to be granted an exploration license. Considering the amendment in Article 17, this guarantee is aimed at ensuring and establishing that the exploration activities are carried out in accordance with the commitments in the exploration project and will be returned to the license holders in cases where the commitments are fulfilled, otherwise it will be recorded as revenue. Imposing an investment guarantee obligation for the issuance of exploration licenses will ensure that mineral exploration activities, which by their nature require significant labor, cost, technical knowledge and equipment, are carried out by more economically and technically competent investors, and that the quality of our country's mineral exploration activities is increased, and in this context, our country's mining potential will be revealed at the highest possible level.
With the amendments in the twelfth paragraph, an exception has been made to the existing rule that the same group of licenses cannot be granted one on top of the other, in terms of the sub-paragraphs containing the 4th group minerals, allowing the mines in these sub-paragraphs to be licensed separately if they are located together in the same area. Allowing the mines in the sub-paragraphs to be licensed separately will both prevent our mines from remaining idle and enable resources to be utilized more efficiently and effectively. It is evaluated that, thanks to the regulation, exploration activities will be carried out more efficiently and effectively, thus production activities will increase, and even if there is no spatial growth in mining areas, the share of mining in the gross national product will increase thanks to the increased productivity.
With the 8th article of the bill, an amendment has been made to the 17th article of the Mining Law, and sanctions such as guarantee income and license cancellation are foreseen in case of failure to carry out mandatory activities during the exploration periods. With this article, the obligations regarding the exploration license period are simplified and the obligation to determine the reserve in three dimensions and quantity until the end of the exploration period is envisaged, so that the exploration activities can be carried out in a qualified manner by experienced people with financial qualifications, and the sanction of foreclosure of the guarantee is envisaged in case of non-compliance with the commitment presented in the project. In order to prepare investment programs realistically and comply with the programs to the maximum extent, it is aimed to impose license cancellation sanctions if the investment program is realized below 50 percent for two consecutive years.
Mineral exploration works require long and arduous efforts, are costly and carry high financial risk. Exploring a mine and exploiting the deposit found to bring it into the economy requires long-term and theoretical studies. In order to fulfill this requirement effectively enough, compelling collateral requirements and sanctions must be foreseen.
With the 9th article of the bill, the Mining Law
With the amendment to be made in Article 24, compliance with the rehabilitation regulations is ensured and license extension conditions are redefined.

With this article, it is envisaged that the issues related to rehabilitation will be rearranged in more detail and specifically, that the minimum production requirement will be determined by regulation in order to ensure compliance with other articles, and that requests for extension of the license period will be deemed appropriate, and that licenses that do not produce production will not be extended. In the current regulation, there is no minimum activity requirement for production to extend the operating license period. For this reason, it is seen that even license holders who do not engage in production can extend their licenses only by fulfilling the formal conditions, documents and procedures. This situation causes mines to remain idle and public resources not to be used efficiently. The expressions in the eleventh and twelfth paragraphs of Article 24 cause confusion in practice, and administrative fines are canceled by judicial decision due to allegations of repeated penalties for the same act. In order to prevent mining areas from being deliberately left idle, a minimum production activity requirement is introduced, and the penalty for duplication in the law is eliminated. By ensuring the unity of terms regarding the rehabilitation fee, it is envisaged that it will form integrity with the contents of the other articles.
With the 10th article of the Law and the 30th article of the Mining Law, it is regulated that areas that have become invalid, abandoned or neglected for any reason should not always be licensed by tender, but should be licensed by application when necessary. With this article, in current cases, the licenses for the 2nd group subparagraph (b) and 4th group mines, which are given by application, become a tender field in cases where the license is abandoned, dropped or canceled without doing any work or making any determination; This sometimes leads to a misperception in the public, describing a large part of the country as a "mining license area for tender". With the offer, the fields related to the 2nd group subparagraph (b) and 4th group mines can be submitted by application or put out to tender. License areas that have been annulled, canceled or abandoned for any reason and that have not been operated will be deleted from the registry and made available for application. Apart from these groups, other group fields will definitely be licensed by tender because the reserves of the fields are visible.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - President, this will never end.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - I swear, I think so.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - I mean, it can't really happen like that, we all read the proposal and know it.
CHAIRMAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, let's wrap things up now.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, in terms of information; Clause (a) of the 1st group mines: Construction, sand gravel. Clause (b): Brick, tile group. Again, the 2nd group is natural rocks, stones (a): Aggregate, calcite, limestone. (b) group: marble, block granite, andesite. (c) group: Rocks ground in integrated facilities. 3rd group minerals: Salt, dissolved salts in seas and lakes. 4th group minerals: Industrial raw materials. Those in group (a) of the 4th group: Mines such as bentonite, lithium, magnesium, boron, trona, phosphate.

Again, energy raw materials in the 4th group: Hard coal, lignite, asphaltite, metallics. Group (c): Gold, silver, platinum, copper, chrome, cobalt, zinc. Radioactive substances: Uranium, thorium, radium. 5th group: Precious stones, such as diamonds and sapphires. I thought I would give information about the search businesses in these and groups as well.
Let me explain this issue and finish: 3213 with the 11th article of the bill.
By adding the following temporary article to Law no. With this article, it is regulated that the transportation of olive trees within the scope of mining activities required for the country's electricity needs, which will be produced in olive growing fields, and the new olive grove field facility will be carried out in accordance with the procedures and principles determined by the Ministry.
In accordance with Article 20 of Law No. 3573, mining activities have not been carried out in olive groves and olive groves within a distance of 3 kilometers since 1995. The lack of definition of olive grove and olive grove area in the legal legislation causes uncertainty in practice. There are various situations such as the presence of olive trees in areas where there are no olive groves registered in the land registry, or olive groves registered but no trees, and even olive trees planted later in the areas where mining activities are carried out prevent mining activities. Preventing mining activities for energy raw materials causes critical situations in terms of the ability of existing thermal power plants to continue their operations and energy supply security.
In our country, most of the minerals used in electricity generation in thermal power plants are located in areas with olive grove vegetation, and the utilization of resources is prevented by Law No. 3573. In developed countries, mining activities in olive groves are not strictly prohibited, and activities are continued in an environmentally friendly manner. For example, Spain and Greece are also within this scope. In line with the public interest, it is necessary to establish a reasonable balance between olive groves and mining, to ensure and increase energy supply security, to meet the need for electricity production, and to preserve diversity in electricity production. In addition, the current practice creates uncertainty by violating vested rights and negatively affects the investment environment.
Scope of the regulation: The regulation is temporary and will be valid for a limited area and will not have a serious negative impact on the olive grove. Electricity production and mining activities will be allowed in areas with olive groves within the boundaries of the map and coordinate list annexed to the law, in line with the public interest. As a condition for granting permission, the transportation of olive trees was taken as a basis, and in cases where they could not be moved, it became mandatory to establish new olive fields of equal size. In order to rehabilitate the areas in question, twice the operating license fee will be collected from the license holder and at least the same number of olive trees will be planted on the area where the mining activity is completed and the area will be turned into an olive grove again.

In this context, treasury real estate can be rented at market value to those whose olive groves have been expropriated, thus ensuring the continuity of the activities of our farmers engaged in olive cultivation. A reasonable balance will be established between energy security and environmental status, and controlled and compensatory mining activities will be allowed based on public interest.
With the article proposed due to the olive grove regulation, there are 82 thousand olive trees in the attached coordinates and areas, and this figure is 4 percent of the existing number of olive trees in Muğla province and 4 per thousand of the number in our country. In addition, there are examples in academic studies showing that these olive trees will produce crops in a short time if they are transported in accordance with the regulation. If it is understood that it cannot be moved, it is necessary to create a new olive garden of equivalent size, and in addition to these sizes, the rehabilitation fee is charged twice as much from these licenses.
The size of the cultivated area of ​​this olive field is 37 thousand hectares. The size of the coordinates of the Yatağan license is 14,100 hectares, and the size and area of ​​the Yeniköy-Kemerköy license is 23 thousand hectares.
Who owns the licenses subject to the coordinates? We can answer these too. Yeniköy and Kemerköy, İçtaş-Limak partnership; Yatağan belongs to the Aydem Holding partnership.
Approximately how many people are employed in thermal power plants that produce olives? 5 thousand people are employed, including those working in power plants and licensed areas. The economic value of the electricity produced by the thermal power plant is 700 million dollars annually.
How many villages are there in the olive area? There are 26 villages and neighborhoods.
How much of Türkiye's electricity production do the thermal power plants subject to the Zeytinlik article cover? These power plants provide 3.5 percent of our country's electricity production.
How much coal is available in the olive grove area?
Are there reserves? It is estimated that the coal reserves in the coordinated area are likely to be 190 million tons. It is 165 million tons in the Yeniköy license and 35 million tons in the Yatağan license. Rehabilitation work has been carried out in these areas before and 4 million trees have been planted for rehabilitation purposes in the Yeniköy license to date, while the number of olive trees planted for rehabilitation purposes in the Yatağan license so far is around 10 thousand.
CHAIRMAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, is it ending?
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - We will finish it, President, I am collecting it.
The total number of trees planted for rehabilitation purposes in Türkiye is over 22 million. This is approx.
(Humming)
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - We are listening to tales from La Fontaine.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, olive groves are important, it is about matter.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Let's quickly wrap things up.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Approximately 183 thousand of these are olive trees.
"How much money has been spent on rehabilitation so far?" 4.5 billion TL. "Is there a privilege for these licenses?" The history of licenses in the coordinated area dates back to the 1960s. The license law here was born before 1995, when the mining ban was introduced in olive groves. For this reason, there are claims that the property rights of these licenses are damaged by mining regulations in olive groves.

"What is the surface area ratio of the existing licenses in Türkiye, what are their numbers?" If we say; exploration license 8.7 percent, operating license 8.76 percent, operating permit 3.1 percent.
As of today, the actual excavation area within the area with operating permission is 1/1000 of the country's surface area.
While there were 89 million olive trees in our country before 2002, today there are 200 million olive trees.
Our country's olive export income is 60 million dollars in 2024.
As a result of the investigations, the coal to be obtained if the article becomes law will take approximately ten years for thermal power plants.
"Do olive trees maintain their productivity if moved?" There are academic studies in practice showing that olive trees can be transported and can return to their former productivity in a short time after being transported.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Has the person who did that academic study ever seen an olive tree?
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Chairman, in the 12th article of the Law and the provisional article 46 of the Mining Law, transitional provisions have been regulated for licenses given as environmental compliance assurance and letters of guarantee. In licenses for which an environmental compliance guarantee and a letter of guarantee have been given, it is obliged to deposit the amount recorded in the letters of guarantee to the MAPEG account within one year from the effective date of the article without any notification. Otherwise, it is stipulated that the letters of guarantee will be converted into cash and transferred to the rehabilitation fee account. It is also stated that the previously collected environmental compliance fee and cash environmental compliance guarantees to be collected within the scope of this article will be transferred to the rehabilitation fee account, and the amounts transferred to the rehabilitation fee account within the scope of the article will be accrued.
Also with the 13th article of the law.
President, wait a minute, let's talk about 13 a little bit.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - You were hit by an olive, my deputy!
MÜZEYYEN ŞEVKİN (Adana) - Will everyone be given unlimited time?
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan, let's complete it now.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - I'm wrapping it up, President, there is one issue.
President, with the 13th article of the law, with the amendment in the table annex (2) of the Mining Law, the calculation methods for license fees are re-determined by taking into account the rehabilitation costs and a 30 percent discount is made on the license fee. The article aims to provide a discount of 30 percent of the operating license fee, taking into account a new regulation that the rehabilitation fee will be collected from the operating license holder every year equal to the operating license fee.
of the law
Article 14 is one of our important articles. Both this article and the 14th article regulate the allocation processes of pasture lands in renewable energy projects.

In order to be able to carry out the process before the relevant administration regarding the change of allocation purpose of pasture areas required for YEKA Projects to be carried out in places declared as Renewable Energy Resource Areas (YEKA) by the Ministry, and to continue the process of changing the purpose of pasture allocation for both these projects and other energy projects and the environmental impact assessment process simultaneously, it is ensured that the EIA report is not required as a prerequisite for applications for change of purpose of pasture allocation.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - A significant transformation is aimed in this process with the new regulation envisaged to be made within the scope of the regulation. It is possible to carry out the procedures for changing the purpose of pasture allocation in the areas determined in YEKA, before the competition is held, by the relevant administrations under the coordination of the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources. In this way, it will be possible to offer pre-qualified, ready-to-use areas to investors, and the applicability of these projects will be significantly increased.
As a result, this change aims to accelerate renewable energy investments and will make a strategic contribution to Turkey's achievement of its energy transformation goals. Energy supply security is a matter of great importance for environmental sustainability and economic development.
Again, with the 15th article of the law, it is aimed that permit applications will be finalized within a maximum of sixty days, the permit obtained during the pre-license period will be valid until the end of the license period, and no additional permit will be required for works at the study stage.
(Noises)
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - It's over, President, it's over, it's almost there.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - It's over, President.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - One or two items left.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - The presentation is one hour and forty-five minutes.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - There are two items left, President.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - We will tell you what he brought.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - In addition, the fact that the current forest permit processes are quite fragmented and time-consuming for projects based on wind and solar energy creates a serious bureaucratic burden for investors. Repetitive permit steps, obtained separately and especially during the associate and undergraduate periods, and obtaining additional permits for activities such as measurement/drilling both prolong the duration of the investment and reduce motivation. For this purpose, the process of obtaining permission again is abolished.
President, it is also in our 16th article.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - 16 is already the end.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, with the addition of a new temporary article to our 16th article.
(CHP MPs making remarks)
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Because the first signature is authentic.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - It is aimed that the zoning and building license procedures for renewable energy facilities based on wind and solar energy can be carried out by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources. An article has been prepared so that the zoning and building license procedures for renewable energy facilities based on wind and solar energy can be carried out by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources.

Zoning plans of energy production facilities according to the legislation; It is approved by the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change, special provincial administrations and relevant local governments, and building licenses and building occupancy permits are essentially issued by local governments. However, in practice, these processes lead to serious delays due to reasons such as inter-institutional coordination deficiencies, technical capacity differences and lack of unity of implementation. Obtaining these approvals and licenses sometimes takes more than a year. For investors, this situation causes processes to become unpredictable and investment schedules to be disrupted. We are making this arrangement to shorten this situation. Within the scope of the regulation, the authorities of municipalities or special administrations are not taken away; alternatively, this authority is given to the Ministry.
With the 17th article, we are changing the duration here by adding a new paragraph to the provisional article 4 of the Law. We are changing “31/12/2020” to “31/12/2030” and “ten years” to “fifteen years”. Within the scope of the mining operation license and permit issued by the Ministry, which will be put into operation from the date of publication of the Law until 31/12/2030, Article IV of Law No. 3213. We are extending this period by applying an 85 percent discount on permit, rent, easement and usage permit fees for fifteen years from the date of license for electricity generation facilities, transportation roads and energy transmission lines, including those to be transferred to TEIAS and distribution companies, up to the point of connection to the system specified in their licenses, where the minerals in Group (b) are used as input.
CHAIRMAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, we showed a lot of tolerance because you were the first signatory, but let's summarize.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, the end.
Similarly, we are introducing a five-year extension here as well. Here, we bring the same extension for renewable energy.
There is a need for regulation, and we are making regulation in this regard as well.
President, I thank everyone for listening patiently.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, I would like to thank our other MPs for listening patiently.
Of course, since the first signatory is important in the new era, we have made some positive discrimination for you. Thank you very much. We know how you work with other teams in legal studies.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Thank you, President.

fields.Subject: Bazı Kanunlarda Değişiklik Yapılmasına Dair Kanun Teklifi (2/3159) münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Sayın Başkanım, kıymetli milletvekilleri, Bakan Yardımcımız, bürokratlarımız, çok değerli sivil toplum örgütlerinin temsilcileri...
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Sivil toplum giremedi içeri, yok.
NERMİN YILDIRIM KARA (Hatay) - Onlar alınmadı içeri.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - ...çok kıymetli basın mensupları ve tüm hazırun; 13/06/2025 tarih ve 95 sayılı Bazı Kanunlarda Değişiklik Yapılmasına Dair Kanun Teklifi'mizi görüşmek üzere toplanmış bulunuyoruz. Hepinizi saygıyla, sevgiyle, hürmetle selamlıyorum.
İsrail'in başta Gazze halkına karşı sürdürdüğü soykırım olmak üzere bölgesel barışı tehdit eden tüm eylemlerini ve son olarak İran'a yönelik saldırısını da kınıyor, bölgede barışın sağlanması ve zulmün bir an önce sona ermesini temenni ediyoruz.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Vallahi siz dışarıdaki zulmü de bitirebilirseniz çok iyi olur.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Türkiye Yüzyılı vizyonu sadece bir kalkınma programı değil, büyük ve güçlü Türkiye idealinin kurumsal, ekonomik, stratejik zeminidir. Bu vizyonun en önemli ayaklarından biri şüphesiz ki enerjide tam bağımsızlık. Enerji güvenliği artık yalnızca ekonomik bir mesele değil, millî egemenliğin, kalkınmanın ve uluslararası rekabet gücünün temelidir. Enerji ve doğal kaynaklar sadece ekonomik değil, aynı zamanda stratejik bağımsızlığın, üretim gücümüzün ve uluslararası rekabet kapasitemizin temel dayanaklarıdır.
Bu teklif yalnızca bir yasal düzenleme değil, Türkiye'nin enerji ve madencilik stratejisinde yeni bir faza geçiştir. Enerji ithalatına 2002 yılından beri 1 trilyon dolar harcadık. 2022 yılında enerji ve madencilik ithalatımız 96,5 milyar dolar iken 2024'te bu rakam 65,6 milyar dolara gerilemiştir.
2002 yılında 31.846 megavat olan kurulu gücümüz 2024 Nisan itibarıyla 118.668 megavata ulaşmıştır. 2035 yılı için de hedeflenen kurulu güç 227.200 megavattır. 2022 yılında
ilan edilen Türkiye Ulusal Enerji Planı'nın mevcut durumda yüzde 45'i olan yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarından elde edilen elektrik üretimi oranının 2035 yılı itibarıyla yüzde 65 seviyesine çıkarılması hedef konulmuştur. Bu hedefin gerçekleştirilmesi, özellikle rüzgâr ve güneş yatırımlarındaki artışa bağlı olup hedefe ulaşılabilmesi için yenilenebilir enerji projelerimizin hızla hayata geçirilmesi gerekmektedir. Teklifle yapılan düzenlemelerle hedefe makul sürelerde ulaşılması amacıyla bu projelere ait izin süreçlerinin daha sade, daha yalın ve süratli bir hâle getirilmesi öngörülmektedir. Yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarına yapılan yatırımlarda ciddi bir artış yakalanmış olmakla birlikte, yeşil dönüşüm süreci kapsamında bu yatırımların daha da artırılarak sürdürülmesi gerekmektedir.
2024 yılı sonu itibarıyla Türkiye'nin toplam kurulu gücü 115,9 gigavata ulaşmış olup bunun 69,1 gigavatı yani yüzde 60'ı yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarından oluşmaktadır. Bunun 19,8 gigavatlık kısmını güneş, 12,8 gigavatlık kısmını ise rüzgâr kaynaklı enerji santralleri oluşturmaktadır. 2024 yılında 349 teravatsaat elektrik üretiminin 159,4 teravatsaati yani yüzde 45'i yenilenebilir kaynaklardan üretilmiştir. Bu sayede yaklaşık 32 milyar metreküp doğal gaz ithalatının önüne geçilmiş ve yaklaşık 12 milyar Amerikan doları tutarında dış ticaret açığı önlenmiştir. Benzer şekilde, aynı üretim ithal kömürle karşılanmış olsaydı 2024 yılı ortalama fiyatlarıyla 7,7 milyar dolar tutarında yaklaşık 68,5 milyon ton kömür ithal edilmiş olacaktı.
Görüldüğü üzere, yenilenebilir kaynak kullanımı yeşil dönüşüm için bir ihtiyaç olmanın yanı sıra enerjide dışa bağımlılığın azaltılarak enerji kaynaklı cari açığın düşürülmesi bakımından daha stratejik bir öneme sahiptir.

OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Ekonomik büyüme, sanayileşme ve nüfus artışına bağlı olarak ekonominin enerji talebi her geçen yıl artmaktadır. 2024 yılında elektrik talebi yaklaşık 349 teravatsaat seviyesinde gerçekleşmiştir. 2010 yılında yaklaşık 211 teravatsaat olan elektrik üretiminin son on dört yıl içerisinde yüzde 60 oranında artmış olması ülkemizin enerji ihtiyacındaki artışı sürekli kılacağını ortaya koymaktadır. Ayrıca, toplam projeksiyonlar elektrik enerjisi ihtiyacının yıllık yüzde 3,5-4 seviyesinde artacağını, 2035 yılı itibarıyla 110 teravatsaat seviyesine erişeceğini göstermektedir. Bu artışı karşılamak ve enerji arz güvenliğini sağlamak için yeterli kaynaklara dayalı, sürdürülebilir ve çeşitli kaynaklardan oluşan bir üretim portföyü hayati önem taşımaktadır. Bu kapsamda yerli ve yenilenebilir kaynaklara dayalı elektrik üretimi Türkiye'nin enerji arz güvenliği politikasında stratejik bir unsur olarak ön plana çıkmaktadır.
2024 yılındaki elektrik üretimi yaklaşık 47,3 teravatsaat; yüzde 14'ü yerli kömür santrallerinden elde edilmiş, bu sayede yaklaşık 9 milyar metreküp doğal gaz ithalatının önüne geçilmiş ve yaklaşık 3,5 milyar Amerikan doları tutarında dış ticaret açığı önlemiştir. Dolayısıyla yerli kömüre dayalı elektrik üretimi sadece teknik bir üretim tercihi değil; aynı zamanda ekonomik istikrar, millî çıkar ve enerji bağımsızlığı açısından da vazgeçilmez bir unsurdur.
