By adding the following temporary article to Law no. With this article, it is regulated that the transportation of olive trees within the scope of mining activities required for the country's electricity needs, which will be produced in olive growing fields, and the new olive grove field facility will be carried out in accordance with the procedures and principles determined by the Ministry.
In accordance with Article 20 of Law No. 3573, mining activities have not been carried out in olive groves and olive groves within a distance of 3 kilometers since 1995. The lack of definition of olive grove and olive grove area in the legal legislation causes uncertainty in practice. There are various situations such as the presence of olive trees in areas where there are no olive groves registered in the land registry, or olive groves registered but no trees, and even olive trees planted later in the areas where mining activities are carried out prevent mining activities. Preventing mining activities for energy raw materials causes critical situations in terms of the ability of existing thermal power plants to continue their operations and energy supply security.
In our country, most of the minerals used in electricity generation in thermal power plants are located in areas with olive grove vegetation, and the utilization of resources is prevented by Law No. 3573. In developed countries, mining activities in olive groves are not strictly prohibited, and activities are continued in an environmentally friendly manner. For example, Spain and Greece are also within this scope. In line with the public interest, it is necessary to establish a reasonable balance between olive groves and mining, to ensure and increase energy supply security, to meet the need for electricity production, and to preserve diversity in electricity production. In addition, the current practice creates uncertainty by violating vested rights and negatively affects the investment environment.
Scope of the regulation: The regulation is temporary and will be valid for a limited area and will not have a serious negative impact on the olive grove. Electricity production and mining activities will be allowed in areas with olive groves within the boundaries of the map and coordinate list annexed to the law, in line with the public interest. As a condition for granting permission, the transportation of olive trees was taken as a basis, and in cases where they could not be moved, it became mandatory to establish new olive fields of equal size. In order to rehabilitate the areas in question, twice the operating license fee will be collected from the license holder and at least the same number of olive trees will be planted on the area where the mining activity is completed and the area will be turned into an olive grove again.

In this context, treasury real estate can be rented at market value to those whose olive groves have been expropriated, thus ensuring the continuity of the activities of our farmers engaged in olive cultivation. A reasonable balance will be established between energy security and environmental status, and controlled and compensatory mining activities will be allowed based on public interest.
With the article proposed due to the olive grove regulation, there are 82 thousand olive trees in the attached coordinates and areas, and this figure is 4 percent of the existing number of olive trees in Muğla province and 4 per thousand of the number in our country. In addition, there are examples in academic studies showing that these olive trees will produce crops in a short time if they are transported in accordance with the regulation. If it is understood that it cannot be moved, it is necessary to create a new olive garden of equivalent size, and in addition to these sizes, the rehabilitation fee is charged twice as much from these licenses.
The size of the cultivated area of ​​this olive field is 37 thousand hectares. The size of the coordinates of the Yatağan license is 14,100 hectares, and the size and area of ​​the Yeniköy-Kemerköy license is 23 thousand hectares.
Who owns the licenses subject to the coordinates? We can answer these too. Yeniköy and Kemerköy, İçtaş-Limak partnership; Yatağan belongs to the Aydem Holding partnership.
Approximately how many people are employed in thermal power plants that produce olives? 5 thousand people are employed, including those working in power plants and licensed areas. The economic value of the electricity produced by the thermal power plant is 700 million dollars annually.
How many villages are there in the olive area? There are 26 villages and neighborhoods.
How much of Türkiye's electricity production do the thermal power plants subject to the Zeytinlik article cover? These power plants provide 3.5 percent of our country's electricity production.
How much coal is available in the olive grove area?
Are there reserves? It is estimated that the coal reserves in the coordinated area are likely to be 190 million tons. It is 165 million tons in the Yeniköy license and 35 million tons in the Yatağan license. Rehabilitation work has been carried out in these areas before and 4 million trees have been planted for rehabilitation purposes in the Yeniköy license to date, while the number of olive trees planted for rehabilitation purposes in the Yatağan license so far is around 10 thousand.
CHAIRMAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, is it ending?
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - We will finish it, President, I am collecting it.
The total number of trees planted for rehabilitation purposes in Türkiye is over 22 million. This is approx.
(Humming)
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - We are listening to tales from La Fontaine.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, olive groves are important, it is about matter.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Let's quickly wrap things up.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Approximately 183 thousand of these are olive trees.
"How much money has been spent on rehabilitation so far?" 4.5 billion TL. "Is there a privilege for these licenses?" The history of licenses in the coordinated area dates back to the 1960s. The license law here was born before 1995, when the mining ban was introduced in olive groves. For this reason, there are claims that the property rights of these licenses are damaged by mining regulations in olive groves.

"What is the surface area ratio of the existing licenses in Türkiye, what are their numbers?" If we say; exploration license 8.7 percent, operating license 8.76 percent, operating permit 3.1 percent.
As of today, the actual excavation area within the area with operating permission is 1/1000 of the country's surface area.
While there were 89 million olive trees in our country before 2002, today there are 200 million olive trees.
Our country's olive export income is 60 million dollars in 2024.
As a result of the investigations, the coal to be obtained if the article becomes law will take approximately ten years for thermal power plants.
"Do olive trees maintain their productivity if moved?" There are academic studies in practice showing that olive trees can be transported and can return to their former productivity in a short time after being transported.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Has the person who did that academic study ever seen an olive tree?
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Chairman, in the 12th article of the Law and the provisional article 46 of the Mining Law, transitional provisions have been regulated for licenses given as environmental compliance assurance and letters of guarantee. In licenses for which an environmental compliance guarantee and a letter of guarantee have been given, it is obliged to deposit the amount recorded in the letters of guarantee to the MAPEG account within one year from the effective date of the article without any notification. Otherwise, it is stipulated that the letters of guarantee will be converted into cash and transferred to the rehabilitation fee account. It is also stated that the previously collected environmental compliance fee and cash environmental compliance guarantees to be collected within the scope of this article will be transferred to the rehabilitation fee account, and the amounts transferred to the rehabilitation fee account within the scope of the article will be accrued.
Also with the 13th article of the law.
President, wait a minute, let's talk about 13 a little bit.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - You were hit by an olive, my deputy!
MÜZEYYEN ŞEVKİN (Adana) - Will everyone be given unlimited time?
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan, let's complete it now.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - I'm wrapping it up, President, there is one issue.
President, with the 13th article of the law, with the amendment in the table annex (2) of the Mining Law, the calculation methods for license fees are re-determined by taking into account the rehabilitation costs and a 30 percent discount is made on the license fee. The article aims to provide a discount of 30 percent of the operating license fee, taking into account a new regulation that the rehabilitation fee will be collected from the operating license holder every year equal to the operating license fee.
of the law
Article 14 is one of our important articles. Both this article and the 14th article regulate the allocation processes of pasture lands in renewable energy projects.

In order to be able to carry out the process before the relevant administration regarding the change of allocation purpose of pasture areas required for YEKA Projects to be carried out in places declared as Renewable Energy Resource Areas (YEKA) by the Ministry, and to continue the process of changing the purpose of pasture allocation for both these projects and other energy projects and the environmental impact assessment process simultaneously, it is ensured that the EIA report is not required as a prerequisite for applications for change of purpose of pasture allocation.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - Mr. Oğuzhan.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - A significant transformation is aimed in this process with the new regulation envisaged to be made within the scope of the regulation. It is possible to carry out the procedures for changing the purpose of pasture allocation in the areas determined in YEKA, before the competition is held, by the relevant administrations under the coordination of the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources. In this way, it will be possible to offer pre-qualified, ready-to-use areas to investors, and the applicability of these projects will be significantly increased.
As a result, this change aims to accelerate renewable energy investments and will make a strategic contribution to Turkey's achievement of its energy transformation goals. Energy supply security is a matter of great importance for environmental sustainability and economic development.
Again, with the 15th article of the law, it is aimed that permit applications will be finalized within a maximum of sixty days, the permit obtained during the pre-license period will be valid until the end of the license period, and no additional permit will be required for works at the study stage.
(Noises)
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - It's over, President, it's over, it's almost there.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - It's over, President.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - One or two items left.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - The presentation is one hour and forty-five minutes.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - There are two items left, President.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - We will tell you what he brought.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - In addition, the fact that the current forest permit processes are quite fragmented and time-consuming for projects based on wind and solar energy creates a serious bureaucratic burden for investors. Repetitive permit steps, obtained separately and especially during the associate and undergraduate periods, and obtaining additional permits for activities such as measurement/drilling both prolong the duration of the investment and reduce motivation. For this purpose, the process of obtaining permission again is abolished.
President, it is also in our 16th article.
AHMET ÇOLAKOĞLU (Zonguldak) - 16 is already the end.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, with the addition of a new temporary article to our 16th article.
(CHP MPs making remarks)
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Because the first signature is authentic.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - It is aimed that the zoning and building license procedures for renewable energy facilities based on wind and solar energy can be carried out by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources. An article has been prepared so that the zoning and building license procedures for renewable energy facilities based on wind and solar energy can be carried out by the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources.

Zoning plans of energy production facilities according to the legislation; It is approved by the Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change, special provincial administrations and relevant local governments, and building licenses and building occupancy permits are essentially issued by local governments. However, in practice, these processes lead to serious delays due to reasons such as inter-institutional coordination deficiencies, technical capacity differences and lack of unity of implementation. Obtaining these approvals and licenses sometimes takes more than a year. For investors, this situation causes processes to become unpredictable and investment schedules to be disrupted. We are making this arrangement to shorten this situation. Within the scope of the regulation, the authorities of municipalities or special administrations are not taken away; alternatively, this authority is given to the Ministry.
With the 17th article, we are changing the duration here by adding a new paragraph to the provisional article 4 of the Law. We are changing “31/12/2020” to “31/12/2030” and “ten years” to “fifteen years”. Within the scope of the mining operation license and permit issued by the Ministry, which will be put into operation from the date of publication of the Law until 31/12/2030, Article IV of Law No. 3213. We are extending this period by applying an 85 percent discount on permit, rent, easement and usage permit fees for fifteen years from the date of license for electricity generation facilities, transportation roads and energy transmission lines, including those to be transferred to TEIAS and distribution companies, up to the point of connection to the system specified in their licenses, where the minerals in Group (b) are used as input.
CHAIRMAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, we showed a lot of tolerance because you were the first signatory, but let's summarize.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, the end.
Similarly, we are introducing a five-year extension here as well. Here, we bring the same extension for renewable energy.
There is a need for regulation, and we are making regulation in this regard as well.
President, I thank everyone for listening patiently.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - Dear Deputy, I would like to thank our other MPs for listening patiently.
Of course, since the first signatory is important in the new era, we have made some positive discrimination for you. Thank you very much. We know how you work with other teams in legal studies.
OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Thank you, President.

records.originalTurkish: OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - Başkanım, ülkemizin mera varlığı 16,6 milyon hektar arazidir, madene tahsis edilen 34 bin hektar arazi, yenilenebilir enerjiye de 12 bin hektar arazi tahsis edilmiştir. Yeni mera yapılan arazi yani mera olmayıp da mera tahsisi yapılan 2 milyon hektar arazi bulunmaktadır. Dolayısıyla, maden ve enerjiye tahsis edilen mera arazisi tüm arazilerin binde 1'i oranındadır ve yeni mera yapılan araziler de 2 milyon hektarın üzerindedir. Köylülerin birçoğu normalde boşta kalan arazilerini mera yapmaktadır bir yatırım olmasın diye.
records.englishTranslation: OĞUZHAN KAYA (Çorum) - President, our country's pasture land is 16.6 million hectares of land, 34 thousand hectares of land is allocated to mining, and 12 thousand hectares of land is allocated to renewable energy. There are 2 million hectares of land where new pasture has been made, that is, land that is not pasture but has been allocated to pasture. Therefore, pasture land allocated to mining and energy is 1 per thousand of all lands, and new pasture lands are over 2 million hectares. Many of the villagers normally turn their idle lands into pasture so that they do not become an investment.
